ingredients.reason: High in saturated fat, which can contribute to increased LDL cholesterol levels when consumed in excess. Its production also raises environmental concerns.
Classified as a possible human carcinogen by the IARC and has shown tumor-promoting effects in animal studies. It can also act as an endocrine disruptor.
ingredients.substitute: Tocopherols (Vitamin E), Rosemary extract, Ascorbic acid (Vitamin C)

ingredients.reason: High in saturated fats, which can contribute to increased LDL cholesterol levels when consumed in excess.

Classified as 'reasonably anticipated to be a human carcinogen' by the U.S. National Toxicology Program. Concerns exist regarding its potential endocrine-disrupting effects and tumor promotion in animal studies at high doses.
High in saturated fats, which can contribute to increased LDL cholesterol levels when consumed in excess, potentially impacting cardiovascular health.
Butylated Hydroxyanisole (BHA) is classified as a possible human carcinogen by some organizations and an endocrine disruptor. Studies have shown tumor promotion in animal models at high doses.
ingredients.substitute: Tocopherols (Vitamin E), Rosemary extract, Ascorbic acid (Vitamin C)

ingredients.reason: High in saturated fat, which can contribute to increased LDL cholesterol levels when consumed in excess. Environmental concerns regarding deforestation are also associated with its production.
Excessive consumption is linked to increased risk of obesity, type 2 diabetes, and non-alcoholic fatty liver disease due to its high fructose content and rapid absorption.
Classified as a possible human carcinogen by some agencies and has been shown to cause tumors in animal studies. It may also act as an endocrine disruptor.
ingredients.substitute: Vitamin E (Tocopherols), Rosemary Extract, Ascorbic Acid

report.inputText: Palm Oil, High Fructose Corn Syrup, BHA
